question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election
Ballots postmarked by June 2, 2026 can arrive through June 9, 2026, so late counting can still shift primary standings
If Steyer fails to finish top two in the primary, he cannot win the general election and the market resolves NO
If Steyer reaches the general election, California's Democratic partisan lean would materially improve his chances against a Republican opponent
Polymarket resolves based on AP, Fox News, and NBC calling the same winner, or official certification if the networks do not converge
latent_variables: Whether late-counted and outstanding ballots disproportionately favor Steyer relative to Becerra and Hilton
How much remaining vote is left to count and where it is concentrated geographically
Whether Steyer can overtake Becerra for second place despite a substantial current deficit
Conditional on making the runoff, whether Steyer would face Hilton or Becerra and how that matchup would alter his general-election odds
Whether any recount, tabulation irregularity, or prolonged delay changes the final certified top-two result

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer is ultimately recognized as the winner of the November 3, 2026 California gubernatorial election by AP, Fox News, and NBC all calling the race for him, or by official certification showing he won.
supporting_evidence: Steyer is an actual declared candidate and his campaign remains active, indicating a live candidacy rather than a hypothetical one. https://www.tomsteyer.com/

Late-May polling showed Steyer plausibly competitive for a top-two slot; Emerson had Becerra 28%, Steyer 22%, Hilton 21%. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-leads-steyer-and-hilton-toss-up-for-second-spot/

Berkeley IGS polling also had Steyer in the top tier, though trailing Becerra and near Hilton, implying a nonzero path before votes were counted. https://igs.berkeley.edu/research/berkeley-igs-poll/2026-berkeley-igs-poll-releases

California continues counting ballots after Election Day, with ballots accepted through June 9 if postmarked by June 2 and certification not until July 10, leaving some residual room for a comeback. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026

New evidence indicates that if late-certified returns lift Steyer into the top two, Becerra would not seek a recount or court challenge and major Democratic organizations would immediately consolidate staff, voter data, and field resources behind Steyer, improving his conditional general-election path.
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results on June 5 show Steyer in third place at 20.2%, behind Steve Hilton at 27.2% and Xavier Becerra at 26.0%. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

NBC's live results estimate roughly 59.6% of expected vote is in, so Steyer's deficits are large relative to the remaining vote pool. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

CBS Los Angeles reported that analyst Paul Mitchell saw late-count patterns that were not especially favorable to Steyer and noted Hilton was doing pretty well among late voters. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

California's top-two rules mean Steyer must first overtake at least one of the current top two; otherwise he cannot appear on the general-election ballot for governor. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/primary-elections-california

Multiple contemporaneous reports described Hilton and Becerra as leading the race while Steyer faced elimination or an increasingly narrow path. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/
KTLA likewise reported Steyer in third place with 20%, behind Hilton and Becerra. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to finish top two in the primary, loses in the general election, withdraws, or another candidate is certified as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State returns show Steyer currently third, a position that would eliminate him under the top-two system. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

The Secretary of State explains that only the top two primary finishers advance to the general election, making primary position the key gating mechanism. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/primary-elections-california

NBC's live results indicate a substantial share of the vote is already counted, making a large reversal less likely. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results
CBS Los Angeles reported expert analysis that the math and late-vote pattern are not on Steyer's side. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

The Daily News framed the question as whether Steyer still had any path to the runoff at all, underscoring how weak his current position is. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/
AP's live California governor primary results track the same contest in which Steyer trails the apparent top two. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/
contradicting_evidence: Pre-primary polling suggested Steyer had a plausible top-two route and was not an irrelevant candidate. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-leads-steyer-and-hilton-toss-up-for-second-spot/
Berkeley IGS also showed Steyer within striking distance of second before the primary. https://igs.berkeley.edu/research/berkeley-igs-poll/2026-berkeley-igs-poll-releases

California's slow ballot-count process means the current unofficial order is not yet final and certification is still weeks away. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026
The official unprocessed-ballot report confirms that outstanding ballots remain to be processed during the canvass period. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/unprocessed-ballots-status

New evidence weakens the NO case modestly by indicating that if Steyer does move into the runoff, Democrats would quickly unify behind him and Becerra would forgo a recount or court fight, reducing post-primary friction and strengthening Steyer's conditional path.

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 2%, therefore yes_prob = 2%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The new evidence does not materially improve Steyer's odds of actually climbing from third into California's top two, which remains the dominant bottleneck. But it does improve his conditional path if he does advance: Becerra declining to seek a recount or court challenge lowers procedural risk, and immediate consolidation of Democratic Party and union resources behind Steyer would make him a stronger general-election contender against Hilton. That combination is enough to nudge the YES probability up modestly from 1% to 2%, while NO remains overwhelmingly likely because Steyer still first needs an unlikely late-count comeback.

question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election
Ballots postmarked by June 2, 2026 can arrive through June 9, 2026, so late counting can still shift primary standings
If Steyer fails to finish top two in the primary, he cannot win the general election and the market resolves NO
If Steyer reaches the general election, California's Democratic partisan lean would materially improve his chances against a Republican opponent
Polymarket resolves based on AP, Fox News, and NBC calling the same winner, or official certification if the networks do not converge
latent_variables: Whether late-counted and outstanding ballots disproportionately favor Steyer relative to Becerra and Hilton
How much remaining vote is left to count and where it is concentrated geographically
Whether Steyer can overtake Becerra for second place despite a substantial current deficit
Conditional on making the runoff, whether Steyer would face Hilton or Becerra and how that matchup would alter his general-election odds
Whether any recount, tabulation irregularity, or prolonged delay changes the final certified top-two result

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer is ultimately recognized as the winner of the November 3, 2026 California gubernatorial election by AP, Fox News, and NBC all calling the race for him, or by official certification showing he won.
supporting_evidence: Steyer is an actual declared candidate and his campaign remains active, indicating a live candidacy rather than a hypothetical one. https://www.tomsteyer.com/

Late-May polling showed Steyer plausibly competitive for a top-two slot; Emerson had Becerra 28%, Steyer 22%, Hilton 21%. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-leads-steyer-and-hilton-toss-up-for-second-spot/

Berkeley IGS polling also had Steyer in the top tier, though trailing Becerra and near Hilton, implying a nonzero path before votes were counted. https://igs.berkeley.edu/research/berkeley-igs-poll/2026-berkeley-igs-poll-releases

California continues counting ballots after Election Day, with ballots accepted through June 9 if postmarked by June 2 and certification not until July 10, leaving some residual room for a comeback. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026

New evidence says California opened an expedited audit after a ballot-export formatting error omitted some cross-county vote-center tallies from the statewide unofficial governor dashboard, and corrections from two large Southern California counties could reprocess tens of thousands of records; if those omitted tallies skew toward metropolitan vote centers where Steyer invested heavily, his path to overtake second place becomes more plausible.
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results on June 5 show Steyer in third place at 20.2%, behind Steve Hilton at 27.2% and Xavier Becerra at 26.0%. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

NBC's live results estimate roughly 59.6% of expected vote is in, so Steyer's deficits are large relative to the remaining vote pool. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

CBS Los Angeles reported that analyst Paul Mitchell saw late-count patterns that were not especially favorable to Steyer and noted Hilton was doing pretty well among late voters. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

California's top-two rules mean Steyer must first overtake at least one of the current top two; otherwise he cannot appear on the general-election ballot for governor. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/primary-elections-california

Multiple contemporaneous reports described Hilton and Becerra as leading the race while Steyer faced elimination or an increasingly narrow path. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/
KTLA likewise reported Steyer in third place with 20%, behind Hilton and Becerra. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

The new audit evidence says the issue did not affect cast ballots themselves, only the statewide unofficial dashboard export, so this is not direct evidence of net new votes for Steyer and may ultimately change standings little or not at all.

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to finish top two in the primary, loses in the general election, withdraws, or another candidate is certified as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State returns show Steyer currently third, a position that would eliminate him under the top-two system. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

The Secretary of State explains that only the top two primary finishers advance to the general election, making primary position the key gating mechanism. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/primary-elections-california

NBC's live results indicate a substantial share of the vote is already counted, making a large reversal less likely. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results
CBS Los Angeles reported expert analysis that the math and late-vote pattern are not on Steyer's side. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

The Daily News framed the question as whether Steyer still had any path to the runoff at all, underscoring how weak his current position is. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/
AP's live California governor primary results track the same contest in which Steyer trails the apparent top two. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

Even after the new evidence, officials said the problem was a formatting error in the unofficial statewide dashboard rather than an issue with cast ballots, so the base case remains that Steyer stays behind unless corrected uploads are both large and unusually favorable to him.
contradicting_evidence: Pre-primary polling suggested Steyer had a plausible top-two route and was not an irrelevant candidate. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-leads-steyer-and-hilton-toss-up-for-second-spot/
Berkeley IGS also showed Steyer within striking distance of second before the primary. https://igs.berkeley.edu/research/berkeley-igs-poll/2026-berkeley-igs-poll-releases

California's slow ballot-count process means the current unofficial order is not yet final and certification is still weeks away. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026
The official unprocessed-ballot report confirms that outstanding ballots remain to be processed during the canvass period. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/unprocessed-ballots-status

New evidence says an expedited audit is underway because some cross-county vote-center tallies were omitted from the statewide unofficial governor dashboard, with tens of thousands of records to be reprocessed in two large Southern California counties, which meaningfully increases the chance that the current third-place standing understates Steyer's actual vote total.

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 4%, therefore yes_prob = 4%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The new evidence modestly improves Steyer's chances because it directly challenges the reliability of the current unofficial statewide standings: an acknowledged formatting error omitted some cross-county vote-center tallies, and two large Southern California counties may need to reprocess tens of thousands of records. That creates a more credible route for Steyer to narrow or potentially erase part of his deficit than ordinary late counting alone. Still, the issue reportedly did not affect cast ballots themselves, only how some tallies appeared in the unofficial dashboard, so the prior evidence that Steyer was running third and faced a steep top-two hurdle remains dominant. I therefore raise YES from 1% to 4%, but NO remains overwhelmingly more likely.

question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election
Ballots postmarked by June 2, 2026 can arrive through June 9, 2026, so late counting can still shift primary standings
If Steyer fails to finish top two in the primary, he cannot win the general election and the market resolves NO
If Steyer reaches the general election, California's Democratic partisan lean would materially improve his chances against a Republican opponent
Polymarket resolves based on AP, Fox News, and NBC calling the same winner, or official certification if the networks do not converge
latent_variables: Whether late-counted and outstanding ballots disproportionately favor Steyer relative to Becerra and Hilton
How much remaining vote is left to count and where it is concentrated geographically
Whether Steyer can overtake Becerra for second place despite a substantial current deficit
Conditional on making the runoff, whether Steyer would face Hilton or Becerra and how that matchup would alter his general-election odds
Whether any recount, tabulation irregularity, or prolonged delay changes the final certified top-two result

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer is ultimately recognized as the winner of the November 3, 2026 California gubernatorial election by AP, Fox News, and NBC all calling the race for him, or by official certification showing he won.
supporting_evidence: Steyer is an actual declared candidate and his campaign remains active, indicating a live candidacy rather than a hypothetical one. https://www.tomsteyer.com/

Late-May polling showed Steyer plausibly competitive for a top-two slot; Emerson had Becerra 28%, Steyer 22%, Hilton 21%. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-leads-steyer-and-hilton-toss-up-for-second-spot/

Berkeley IGS polling also had Steyer in the top tier, though trailing Becerra and near Hilton, implying a nonzero path before votes were counted. https://igs.berkeley.edu/research/berkeley-igs-poll/2026-berkeley-igs-poll-releases

California continues counting ballots after Election Day, with ballots accepted through June 9 if postmarked by June 2 and certification not until July 10, leaving some residual room for a comeback. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results on June 5 show Steyer in third place at 20.2%, behind Steve Hilton at 27.2% and Xavier Becerra at 26.0%. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

NBC's live results estimate roughly 59.6% of expected vote is in, so Steyer's deficits are large relative to the remaining vote pool. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

CBS Los Angeles reported that analyst Paul Mitchell saw late-count patterns that were not especially favorable to Steyer and noted Hilton was doing pretty well among late voters. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

California's top-two rules mean Steyer must first overtake at least one of the current top two; otherwise he cannot appear on the general-election ballot for governor. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/primary-elections-california

Multiple contemporaneous reports described Hilton and Becerra as leading the race while Steyer faced elimination or an increasingly narrow path. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/
KTLA likewise reported Steyer in third place with 20%, behind Hilton and Becerra. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

New evidence indicates about 1.1 million ballots remained, over 70% in Los Angeles County and Bay Area counties where Becerra was already running ahead of Steyer; Political Data Inc. estimated Steyer would need to win outstanding ballots by about 9 points statewide, but late-counted batches had instead favored Becerra by about 6 points, sharply worsening Steyer's already narrow runoff path.

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to finish top two in the primary, loses in the general election, withdraws, or another candidate is certified as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State returns show Steyer currently third, a position that would eliminate him under the top-two system. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

The Secretary of State explains that only the top two primary finishers advance to the general election, making primary position the key gating mechanism. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/primary-elections-california

NBC's live results indicate a substantial share of the vote is already counted, making a large reversal less likely. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results
CBS Los Angeles reported expert analysis that the math and late-vote pattern are not on Steyer's side. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

The Daily News framed the question as whether Steyer still had any path to the runoff at all, underscoring how weak his current position is. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/
AP's live California governor primary results track the same contest in which Steyer trails the apparent top two. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

New evidence indicates the remaining ballot pool is concentrated in areas favoring Becerra over Steyer, and that Steyer would need roughly a 9-point edge on outstanding ballots while recent late-counted batches have instead favored Becerra by about 6 points, making a comeback into second place materially less likely.
contradicting_evidence: Pre-primary polling suggested Steyer had a plausible top-two route and was not an irrelevant candidate. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-leads-steyer-and-hilton-toss-up-for-second-spot/
Berkeley IGS also showed Steyer within striking distance of second before the primary. https://igs.berkeley.edu/research/berkeley-igs-poll/2026-berkeley-igs-poll-releases

California's slow ballot-count process means the current unofficial order is not yet final and certification is still weeks away. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026
The official unprocessed-ballot report confirms that outstanding ballots remain to be processed during the canvass period. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/unprocessed-ballots-status

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 0.3%, therefore yes_prob = 0.3%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The new evidence makes Steyer's late-count comeback path substantially weaker than before. Previously, the small residual YES probability reflected generic uncertainty from California's slow count. Now there is more specific directional information: the outstanding ballots are concentrated in places where Becerra is already outperforming Steyer, Steyer reportedly needs an implausibly large margin on the remaining vote to pass Becerra, and the late batches counted so far have moved the wrong way for him. Because Steyer still must first reach the top two before he can have any path to winning in November, this evidence pushes the forecast further toward NO and reduces the residual YES chance from 1% to 0.3%.

question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election
Ballots postmarked by June 2, 2026 can arrive through June 9, 2026, so late counting can still shift primary standings
If Steyer fails to finish top two in the primary, he cannot win the general election and the market resolves NO
If Steyer reaches the general election, California's Democratic partisan lean would materially improve his chances against a Republican opponent
Polymarket resolves based on AP, Fox News, and NBC calling the same winner, or official certification if the networks do not converge
latent_variables: Whether late-counted and outstanding ballots disproportionately favor Steyer relative to Becerra and Hilton
How much remaining vote is left to count and where it is concentrated geographically
Whether Steyer can overtake Becerra for second place despite a substantial current deficit
Conditional on making the runoff, whether Steyer would face Hilton or Becerra and how that matchup would alter his general-election odds
Whether any recount, tabulation irregularity, or prolonged delay changes the final certified top-two result

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer is ultimately recognized as the winner of the November 3, 2026 California gubernatorial election by AP, Fox News, and NBC all calling the race for him, or by official certification showing he won.
supporting_evidence: Steyer is an actual declared candidate and his campaign remains active, indicating a live candidacy rather than a hypothetical one. https://www.tomsteyer.com/

Late-May polling showed Steyer plausibly competitive for a top-two slot; Emerson had Becerra 28%, Steyer 22%, Hilton 21%. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-leads-steyer-and-hilton-toss-up-for-second-spot/

Berkeley IGS polling also had Steyer in the top tier, though trailing Becerra and near Hilton, implying a nonzero path before votes were counted. https://igs.berkeley.edu/research/berkeley-igs-poll/2026-berkeley-igs-poll-releases

California continues counting ballots after Election Day, with ballots accepted through June 9 if postmarked by June 2 and certification not until July 10, leaving some residual room for a comeback. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results on June 5 show Steyer in third place at 20.2%, behind Steve Hilton at 27.2% and Xavier Becerra at 26.0%. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

NBC's live results estimate roughly 59.6% of expected vote is in, so Steyer's deficits are large relative to the remaining vote pool. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

CBS Los Angeles reported that analyst Paul Mitchell saw late-count patterns that were not especially favorable to Steyer and noted Hilton was doing pretty well among late voters. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

California's top-two rules mean Steyer must first overtake at least one of the current top two; otherwise he cannot appear on the general-election ballot for governor. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/primary-elections-california

Multiple contemporaneous reports described Hilton and Becerra as leading the race while Steyer faced elimination or an increasingly narrow path. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/
KTLA likewise reported Steyer in third place with 20%, behind Hilton and Becerra. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

New evidence: on June 6, Steyer said he would not request a recount or file any court challenge, acknowledged no realistic path into the top two, and said his campaign would respect the canvass; election lawyers said this sharply reduced the odds of any post-election process changing the ordering before certification.

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to finish top two in the primary, loses in the general election, withdraws, or another candidate is certified as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State returns show Steyer currently third, a position that would eliminate him under the top-two system. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

The Secretary of State explains that only the top two primary finishers advance to the general election, making primary position the key gating mechanism. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/primary-elections-california

NBC's live results indicate a substantial share of the vote is already counted, making a large reversal less likely. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results
CBS Los Angeles reported expert analysis that the math and late-vote pattern are not on Steyer's side. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

The Daily News framed the question as whether Steyer still had any path to the runoff at all, underscoring how weak his current position is. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/
AP's live California governor primary results track the same contest in which Steyer trails the apparent top two. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

New evidence: on June 6, Steyer said he would not request a recount or file any court challenge and acknowledged no realistic path into the top two; election lawyers said that decision sharply reduced the chance of any post-election process changing the candidate order before certification.
contradicting_evidence: Pre-primary polling suggested Steyer had a plausible top-two route and was not an irrelevant candidate. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-leads-steyer-and-hilton-toss-up-for-second-spot/
Berkeley IGS also showed Steyer within striking distance of second before the primary. https://igs.berkeley.edu/research/berkeley-igs-poll/2026-berkeley-igs-poll-releases

California's slow ballot-count process means the current unofficial order is not yet final and certification is still weeks away. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026
The official unprocessed-ballot report confirms that outstanding ballots remain to be processed during the canvass period. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/unprocessed-ballots-status

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 0.2%, therefore yes_prob = 0.2%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The new evidence primarily removes one of the last residual upside paths for Steyer: a recount or court-driven post-election reversal. In the initial forecast, the remaining 1% YES probability came mostly from uncertified results, late ballot counting, and a small chance that recounts, challenges, or tabulation anomalies could alter the top-two ordering. Steyer's own decision not to pursue a recount or legal challenge, combined with his acknowledgment that he has no realistic path, makes that already narrow escape route materially less plausible. Some tiny residual probability remains because canvassing is still ongoing and uncertified results can still move organically, but the odds of Steyer ultimately winning now look even closer to zero.

question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election
Ballots postmarked by June 2, 2026 can arrive through June 9, 2026, so late counting can still shift primary standings
If Steyer fails to finish top two in the primary, he cannot win the general election and the market resolves NO
If Steyer reaches the general election, California's Democratic partisan lean would materially improve his chances against a Republican opponent
Polymarket resolves based on AP, Fox News, and NBC calling the same winner, or official certification if the networks do not converge
latent_variables: Whether late-counted and outstanding ballots disproportionately favor Steyer relative to Becerra and Hilton
How much remaining vote is left to count and where it is concentrated geographically
Whether Steyer can overtake Becerra for second place despite a substantial current deficit
Conditional on making the runoff, whether Steyer would face Hilton or Becerra and how that matchup would alter his general-election odds
Whether any recount, tabulation irregularity, or prolonged delay changes the final certified top-two result

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer is ultimately recognized as the winner of the November 3, 2026 California gubernatorial election by AP, Fox News, and NBC all calling the race for him, or by official certification showing he won.
supporting_evidence: Steyer is an actual declared candidate and his campaign remains active, indicating a live candidacy rather than a hypothetical one. https://www.tomsteyer.com/

Late-May polling showed Steyer plausibly competitive for a top-two slot; Emerson had Becerra 28%, Steyer 22%, Hilton 21%. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-leads-steyer-and-hilton-toss-up-for-second-spot/

Berkeley IGS polling also had Steyer in the top tier, though trailing Becerra and near Hilton, implying a nonzero path before votes were counted. https://igs.berkeley.edu/research/berkeley-igs-poll/2026-berkeley-igs-poll-releases

California continues counting ballots after Election Day, with ballots accepted through June 9 if postmarked by June 2 and certification not until July 10, leaving some residual room for a comeback. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results on June 5 show Steyer in third place at 20.2%, behind Steve Hilton at 27.2% and Xavier Becerra at 26.0%. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

NBC's live results estimate roughly 59.6% of expected vote is in, so Steyer's deficits are large relative to the remaining vote pool. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

CBS Los Angeles reported that analyst Paul Mitchell saw late-count patterns that were not especially favorable to Steyer and noted Hilton was doing pretty well among late voters. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

California's top-two rules mean Steyer must first overtake at least one of the current top two; otherwise he cannot appear on the general-election ballot for governor. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/primary-elections-california

Multiple contemporaneous reports described Hilton and Becerra as leading the race while Steyer faced elimination or an increasingly narrow path. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/
KTLA likewise reported Steyer in third place with 20%, behind Hilton and Becerra. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

New county-level canvass evidence indicates the deadline to cure signature and identification problems has effectively passed, so most remaining work is merely tabulating already-accepted ballots rather than adding newly salvaged ballots, further shrinking Steyer's comeback avenues through ballot-curing operations.

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to finish top two in the primary, loses in the general election, withdraws, or another candidate is certified as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State returns show Steyer currently third, a position that would eliminate him under the top-two system. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

The Secretary of State explains that only the top two primary finishers advance to the general election, making primary position the key gating mechanism. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/primary-elections-california

NBC's live results indicate a substantial share of the vote is already counted, making a large reversal less likely. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results
CBS Los Angeles reported expert analysis that the math and late-vote pattern are not on Steyer's side. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

The Daily News framed the question as whether Steyer still had any path to the runoff at all, underscoring how weak his current position is. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/
AP's live California governor primary results track the same contest in which Steyer trails the apparent top two. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

New canvass-stage reporting says cure deadlines for signature mismatches and missing ID have expired in key counties and that remaining work is mostly administrative tabulation of already-accepted ballots, limiting campaigns' ability to expand totals through voter-contact efforts.
contradicting_evidence: Pre-primary polling suggested Steyer had a plausible top-two route and was not an irrelevant candidate. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-leads-steyer-and-hilton-toss-up-for-second-spot/
Berkeley IGS also showed Steyer within striking distance of second before the primary. https://igs.berkeley.edu/research/berkeley-igs-poll/2026-berkeley-igs-poll-releases

California's slow ballot-count process means the current unofficial order is not yet final and certification is still weeks away. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026
The official unprocessed-ballot report confirms that outstanding ballots remain to be processed during the canvass period. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/unprocessed-ballots-status

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 0.5%, therefore yes_prob = 0.5%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The new evidence makes Steyer's already narrow path even smaller. Previously, a tiny residual chance remained because slow California counting and ballot-curing operations left some room for campaigns to add votes at the margins. The update says those cure deadlines have now expired in major counties and that most remaining activity is administrative tabulation of ballots already accepted, not rescuing rejected ballots. That reduces the scope for Steyer to generate new gains through canvassing or voter contact, so the remaining upside is mostly limited to ordinary variance in the ballots still being tabulated rather than any meaningful expansion of the eligible vote pool.

question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election
Ballots postmarked by June 2, 2026 can arrive through June 9, 2026, so late counting can still shift primary standings
If Steyer fails to finish top two in the primary, he cannot win the general election and the market resolves NO
If Steyer reaches the general election, California's Democratic partisan lean would materially improve his chances against a Republican opponent
Polymarket resolves based on AP, Fox News, and NBC calling the same winner, or official certification if the networks do not converge
latent_variables: Whether late-counted and outstanding ballots disproportionately favor Steyer relative to Becerra and Hilton
How much remaining vote is left to count and where it is concentrated geographically
Whether Steyer can overtake Becerra for second place despite a substantial current deficit
Conditional on making the runoff, whether Steyer would face Hilton or Becerra and how that matchup would alter his general-election odds
Whether any recount, tabulation irregularity, or prolonged delay changes the final certified top-two result

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer is ultimately recognized as the winner of the November 3, 2026 California gubernatorial election by AP, Fox News, and NBC all calling the race for him, or by official certification showing he won.
supporting_evidence: Steyer is an actual declared candidate and his campaign remains active, indicating a live candidacy rather than a hypothetical one. https://www.tomsteyer.com/

Late-May polling showed Steyer plausibly competitive for a top-two slot; Emerson had Becerra 28%, Steyer 22%, Hilton 21%. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-leads-steyer-and-hilton-toss-up-for-second-spot/

Berkeley IGS polling also had Steyer in the top tier, though trailing Becerra and near Hilton, implying a nonzero path before votes were counted. https://igs.berkeley.edu/research/berkeley-igs-poll/2026-berkeley-igs-poll-releases

California continues counting ballots after Election Day, with ballots accepted through June 9 if postmarked by June 2 and certification not until July 10, leaving some residual room for a comeback. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results on June 5 show Steyer in third place at 20.2%, behind Steve Hilton at 27.2% and Xavier Becerra at 26.0%. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

NBC's live results estimate roughly 59.6% of expected vote is in, so Steyer's deficits are large relative to the remaining vote pool. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

CBS Los Angeles reported that analyst Paul Mitchell saw late-count patterns that were not especially favorable to Steyer and noted Hilton was doing pretty well among late voters. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

California's top-two rules mean Steyer must first overtake at least one of the current top two; otherwise he cannot appear on the general-election ballot for governor. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/primary-elections-california

Multiple contemporaneous reports described Hilton and Becerra as leading the race while Steyer faced elimination or an increasingly narrow path. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/
KTLA likewise reported Steyer in third place with 20%, behind Hilton and Becerra. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

The new June 6 administrative update says the state is only delaying nightly posting of county statement-of-vote PDFs because of maintenance, while canvassing continues on the normal timetable, so it does not create substantive new upside for Steyer.

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to finish top two in the primary, loses in the general election, withdraws, or another candidate is certified as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State returns show Steyer currently third, a position that would eliminate him under the top-two system. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

The Secretary of State explains that only the top two primary finishers advance to the general election, making primary position the key gating mechanism. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/primary-elections-california

NBC's live results indicate a substantial share of the vote is already counted, making a large reversal less likely. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results
CBS Los Angeles reported expert analysis that the math and late-vote pattern are not on Steyer's side. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

The Daily News framed the question as whether Steyer still had any path to the runoff at all, underscoring how weak his current position is. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/
AP's live California governor primary results track the same contest in which Steyer trails the apparent top two. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

The new June 6 notice says the only change is a temporary delay in posting county statement-of-vote PDFs, while counties continue canvassing under the normal timetable, which suggests a routine administrative issue rather than any development that improves Steyer's electoral position.
contradicting_evidence: Pre-primary polling suggested Steyer had a plausible top-two route and was not an irrelevant candidate. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-leads-steyer-and-hilton-toss-up-for-second-spot/
Berkeley IGS also showed Steyer within striking distance of second before the primary. https://igs.berkeley.edu/research/berkeley-igs-poll/2026-berkeley-igs-poll-releases

California's slow ballot-count process means the current unofficial order is not yet final and certification is still weeks away. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026
The official unprocessed-ballot report confirms that outstanding ballots remain to be processed during the canvass period. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/unprocessed-ballots-status

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 1%, therefore yes_prob = 1%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The new evidence is operational rather than electoral: it only delays the nightly public posting of county statement-of-vote PDFs and explicitly says canvassing continues on the normal timetable. That slightly updates the process model around public visibility of supporting documents, but it does not improve Steyer's chances of making the top two or winning the governorship. So the substantive forecast is unchanged: Steyer still appears overwhelmingly likely to lose, with only a small residual probability tied to the ongoing count and uncertified status.

question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election
Ballots postmarked by June 2, 2026 can arrive through June 9, 2026, so late counting can still shift primary standings
If Steyer fails to finish top two in the primary, he cannot win the general election and the market resolves NO
If Steyer reaches the general election, California's Democratic partisan lean would materially improve his chances against a Republican opponent
Polymarket resolves based on AP, Fox News, and NBC calling the same winner, or official certification if the networks do not converge
latent_variables: Whether late-counted and outstanding ballots disproportionately favor Steyer relative to Becerra and Hilton
How much remaining vote is left to count and where it is concentrated geographically
Whether Steyer can overtake Becerra for second place despite a substantial current deficit
Conditional on making the runoff, whether Steyer would face Hilton or Becerra and how that matchup would alter his general-election odds
Whether any recount, tabulation irregularity, or prolonged delay changes the final certified top-two result

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer is ultimately recognized as the winner of the November 3, 2026 California gubernatorial election by AP, Fox News, and NBC all calling the race for him, or by official certification showing he won.
supporting_evidence: Steyer is an actual declared candidate and his campaign remains active, indicating a live candidacy rather than a hypothetical one. https://www.tomsteyer.com/

Late-May polling showed Steyer plausibly competitive for a top-two slot; Emerson had Becerra 28%, Steyer 22%, Hilton 21%. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-leads-steyer-and-hilton-toss-up-for-second-spot/

Berkeley IGS polling also had Steyer in the top tier, though trailing Becerra and near Hilton, implying a nonzero path before votes were counted. https://igs.berkeley.edu/research/berkeley-igs-poll/2026-berkeley-igs-poll-releases

California continues counting ballots after Election Day, with ballots accepted through June 9 if postmarked by June 2 and certification not until July 10, leaving some residual room for a comeback. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results on June 5 show Steyer in third place at 20.2%, behind Steve Hilton at 27.2% and Xavier Becerra at 26.0%. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

NBC's live results estimate roughly 59.6% of expected vote is in, so Steyer's deficits are large relative to the remaining vote pool. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

CBS Los Angeles reported that analyst Paul Mitchell saw late-count patterns that were not especially favorable to Steyer and noted Hilton was doing pretty well among late voters. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

California's top-two rules mean Steyer must first overtake at least one of the current top two; otherwise he cannot appear on the general-election ballot for governor. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/primary-elections-california

Multiple contemporaneous reports described Hilton and Becerra as leading the race while Steyer faced elimination or an increasingly narrow path. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/
KTLA likewise reported Steyer in third place with 20%, behind Hilton and Becerra. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

The new NBC evidence concerns internal decision-desk staffing and explicitly says the reorganization did not alter election-calling criteria, so it does not improve Steyer's substantive path to being recognized as the winner.

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to finish top two in the primary, loses in the general election, withdraws, or another candidate is certified as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State returns show Steyer currently third, a position that would eliminate him under the top-two system. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

The Secretary of State explains that only the top two primary finishers advance to the general election, making primary position the key gating mechanism. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/primary-elections-california

NBC's live results indicate a substantial share of the vote is already counted, making a large reversal less likely. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results
CBS Los Angeles reported expert analysis that the math and late-vote pattern are not on Steyer's side. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

The Daily News framed the question as whether Steyer still had any path to the runoff at all, underscoring how weak his current position is. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/
AP's live California governor primary results track the same contest in which Steyer trails the apparent top two. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

The new NBC evidence is operational rather than electoral: NBC said the decision-desk reorganization did not change its criteria for calling races, so it does not create a new route for Steyer to win or be called the winner.
contradicting_evidence: Pre-primary polling suggested Steyer had a plausible top-two route and was not an irrelevant candidate. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-leads-steyer-and-hilton-toss-up-for-second-spot/
Berkeley IGS also showed Steyer within striking distance of second before the primary. https://igs.berkeley.edu/research/berkeley-igs-poll/2026-berkeley-igs-poll-releases

California's slow ballot-count process means the current unofficial order is not yet final and certification is still weeks away. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026
The official unprocessed-ballot report confirms that outstanding ballots remain to be processed during the canvass period. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/unprocessed-ballots-status

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 1%, therefore yes_prob = 1%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The new evidence does not materially change the forecast. It describes an NBC internal staffing and management change for decision-desk operations, but NBC explicitly said its election-calling criteria were unchanged. Because the prior forecast already depended overwhelmingly on Steyer's weak electoral position in the top-two primary rather than on any quirks in network call procedures, this update is effectively neutral. Steyer still appears to need an unlikely late-count comeback merely to reach the general election, so the YES probability remains 1%.

NEW YORK, June 6 — NBC News has appointed veteran election producer Marisa Chen as senior director for West Coast decision-desk operations ahead of this year's California general election coverage, according to an internal memo reviewed by reporters on Saturday. Late-May polling showed Steyer plausibly competitive for a top-two slot; Emerson had Becerra 28%, Steyer 22%, Hilton 21%. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-leads-steyer-and-hilton-toss-up-for-second-spot/

Berkeley IGS polling also had Steyer in the top tier, though trailing Becerra and near Hilton, implying a nonzero path before votes were counted. https://igs.berkeley.edu/research/berkeley-igs-poll/2026-berkeley-igs-poll-releases

California continues counting ballots after Election Day, with ballots accepted through June 9 if postmarked by June 2 and certification not until July 10, leaving some residual room for a comeback. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results on June 5 show Steyer in third place at 20.2%, behind Steve Hilton at 27.2% and Xavier Becerra at 26.0%. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

NBC's live results estimate roughly 59.6% of expected vote is in, so Steyer's deficits are large relative to the remaining vote pool. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

CBS Los Angeles reported that analyst Paul Mitchell saw late-count patterns that were not especially favorable to Steyer and noted Hilton was doing pretty well among late voters. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

California's top-two rules mean Steyer must first overtake at least one of the current top two; otherwise he cannot appear on the general-election ballot for governor. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/primary-elections-california

Multiple contemporaneous reports described Hilton and Becerra as leading the race while Steyer faced elimination or an increasingly narrow path. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/
KTLA likewise reported Steyer in third place with 20%, behind Hilton and Becerra. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

The new Sacramento booking evidence is explicitly described as routine election-cycle planning that does not indicate which candidates are likely to advance or win, so it provides no meaningful new support for a Steyer comeback.

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to finish top two in the primary, loses in the general election, withdraws, or another candidate is certified as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State returns show Steyer currently third, a position that would eliminate him under the top-two system. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

The Secretary of State explains that only the top two primary finishers advance to the general election, making primary position the key gating mechanism. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/primary-elections-california

NBC's live results indicate a substantial share of the vote is already counted, making a large reversal less likely. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results
CBS Los Angeles reported expert analysis that the math and late-vote pattern are not on Steyer's side. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

The Daily News framed the question as whether Steyer still had any path to the runoff at all, underscoring how weak his current position is. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/
AP's live California governor primary results track the same contest in which Steyer trails the apparent top two. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

The new Sacramento booking evidence describes only routine planning by campaigns, advocacy groups, and media for the fall race and explicitly says it does not reflect any view about which candidates are likely to advance or win, so it leaves the existing anti-Steyer evidence essentially unchanged.
contradicting_evidence: Pre-primary polling suggested Steyer had a plausible top-two route and was not an irrelevant candidate. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-leads-steyer-and-hilton-toss-up-for-second-spot/
Berkeley IGS also showed Steyer within striking distance of second before the primary. https://igs.berkeley.edu/research/berkeley-igs-poll/2026-berkeley-igs-poll-releases

California's slow ballot-count process means the current unofficial order is not yet final and certification is still weeks away. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026
The official unprocessed-ballot report confirms that outstanding ballots remain to be processed during the canvass period. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/unprocessed-ballots-status

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 1%, therefore yes_prob = 1%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The new evidence is operationally neutral. It indicates normal Sacramento election-season spending and planning for a November governor's race, but it explicitly says those bookings do not signal which candidates are likely to advance or win. That does not materially change Steyer's core problem: he is still described in the prior forecast as trailing in third place under a top-two system. Therefore the forecast remains essentially unchanged at 1% YES and 99% NO.

question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC decision desks
Campaign donors, endorsers, unions, and party networks
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election regardless of party
Steyer must first finish in the top two statewide in the June 2, 2026 primary canvass
If Steyer advances, he must then win the November 3, 2026 gubernatorial general election
The market resolves when AP, Fox News, and NBC all call the race for the same candidate, or else by official certification
If the election is not confirmed by July 31, 2027, the market resolves to Other, which is effectively NO for this binary market
latent_variables: How many uncounted ballots remain and whether their partisan/geographic mix favors Steyer disproportionately
Whether Steyer can overtake Becerra or Hilton during the canvass to claim a top-two spot
General-election strength of Steyer conditional on making the runoff
Late endorsements, legal challenges, recount dynamics, or candidate withdrawals
How California's Democratic lean interacts with the eventual two-candidate runoff matchup

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election, either after advancing from the June 2, 2026 top-two primary and then winning the November 3, 2026 general election, with AP/Fox/NBC all calling the race for him or official certification establishing him as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Steyer is a real, active candidate with an official campaign site stating he is running for Governor of California in 2026. https://www.tomsteyer.com/meet-tom

NBC Los Angeles reported Steyer had spent more than $100 million on the campaign, indicating serious capacity and organization. https://www.nbclosangeles.com/news/local/who-is-tom-steyer-california-governor-race-election/3883007/

Some recent coverage said Steyer was not conceding and still arguing he had a path to the November runoff while ballots were being counted. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/

Pre-primary polls showed Steyer in the top tier, though not clearly ahead; Emerson had him tied for second in one survey. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-continues-to-surge-steyer-and-hilton-compete-for-second-spot/

New June 6 evidence says late-counted vote-by-mail and cured ballots have broken heavily toward Steyer, cutting his gap to second place to about 0.4 points, or roughly 28,000 votes, with an estimated 1.1 million ballots still outstanding.
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results show the June 2, 2026 primary is underway in the canvass period and only the top two will advance, creating a hard gate before November. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor
Recent result coverage consistently placed Steyer third behind Hilton and Becerra as counting continued. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

KTLA reported Hilton around 28%, Becerra around 26%, and Steyer around 20% with roughly 56% counted, leaving Steyer materially behind for a top-two place. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

Friday-update reporting said there were no major shifts in the governor race as ballots continued to be counted. https://www.desertsun.com/story/news/nation/california/2026/06/04/california-primary-results-latest-governor-los-angeles-mayor-house/90403103007/

The June 1 CEPP poll had Becerra 29%, Hilton 23%, and Steyer 18% after undecideds were pushed, matching the broad pattern that Steyer was competitive but not leading. https://www.cpp.edu/class/political-science/docs/media/cepp_2026_california_primary_poll_results_june_1_2026.pdf

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to make the top two in the June 2, 2026 primary, loses the November 3, 2026 general election, withdraws, or the market resolves to Other because the result is not confirmed by July 31, 2027.
supporting_evidence: California uses a top-two primary, so finishing third is functionally near-elimination because only two candidates advance to November. https://ballotpedia.org/California_gubernatorial_election%2C_2026_%28June_2_top-two_primary%29

Official state results show the primary remains unofficial pending certification, meaning the immediate bottleneck is the canvass rather than the general election. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

Multiple outlets reported Hilton and Becerra holding the top two positions while Steyer trailed in third as counting continued. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

NPR described the contest as Democrats and Republicans vying for the top two, with Steyer one of the candidates in close contention but not in the lead. https://www.npr.org/2026/06/03/nx-s1-5844747/california-primary-election-results-governor

OC Register reported Hilton and Becerra positioned in the top two spots and expressing confidence that their standings would hold. https://www.ocregister.com/2026/06/02/2026-election-results-californias-governor-race-becerra-steyer-hilton-face-off-against-58-others/

A NewsNation/DDHQ result page said Decision Desk HQ projected Becerra would advance, further narrowing Steyer's path. https://www.newsnationnow.com/politics/2026-midterm-elections/california-governor-race-primary-results-2026/
contradicting_evidence: The Secretary of State notes the results are still unofficial and will not be certified until July 10, 2026, so a late-count reversal is still technically possible. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026

California counts ballots slowly after election day, and national/live result pages continued to show the race as not fully called during the canvass. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

California's strong Democratic lean means that if Steyer somehow reaches the general election, he could still be viable in some runoff matchups. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/report-registration/15day-primary-2026

New June 6 evidence says late-counted ballots have favored Steyer enough to cut the gap to second place to about 0.4 points, leaving him within roughly 28,000 votes with a large outstanding ballot pool still to be counted.

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 11%, therefore yes_prob = 11%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The key change is that Steyer's path through the top-two primary bottleneck is no longer a remote long shot: the new June 6 evidence says late-counted ballots are breaking sharply toward him, shrinking his deficit for second place to about 0.4 points, or roughly 28,000 votes. That materially raises the chance that he overtakes Becerra during the canvass. However, he is still currently in third, still must actually finish in the top two, and would still need to win the November 3, 2026 general election afterward. So the forecast moves up meaningfully, but NO remains more likely than YES.

question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC decision desks
Campaign donors, endorsers, unions, and party networks
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election regardless of party
Steyer must first finish in the top two statewide in the June 2, 2026 primary canvass
If Steyer advances, he must then win the November 3, 2026 gubernatorial general election
The market resolves when AP, Fox News, and NBC all call the race for the same candidate, or else by official certification
If the election is not confirmed by July 31, 2027, the market resolves to Other, which is effectively NO for this binary market
latent_variables: How many uncounted ballots remain and whether their partisan/geographic mix favors Steyer disproportionately
Whether Steyer can overtake Becerra or Hilton during the canvass to claim a top-two spot
General-election strength of Steyer conditional on making the runoff
Late endorsements, legal challenges, recount dynamics, or candidate withdrawals
How California's Democratic lean interacts with the eventual two-candidate runoff matchup

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election, either after advancing from the June 2, 2026 top-two primary and then winning the November 3, 2026 general election, with AP/Fox/NBC all calling the race for him or official certification establishing him as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Steyer is a real, active candidate with an official campaign site stating he is running for Governor of California in 2026. https://www.tomsteyer.com/meet-tom

NBC Los Angeles reported Steyer had spent more than $100 million on the campaign, indicating serious capacity and organization. https://www.nbclosangeles.com/news/local/who-is-tom-steyer-california-governor-race-election/3883007/

Some recent coverage said Steyer was not conceding and still arguing he had a path to the November runoff while ballots were being counted. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/

Pre-primary polls showed Steyer in the top tier, though not clearly ahead; Emerson had him tied for second in one survey. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-continues-to-surge-steyer-and-hilton-compete-for-second-spot/

New evidence indicates that if late-certified returns lift Steyer into the top two, Becerra would not seek a recount or court challenge and top California Democratic Party and union leaders would immediately consolidate staff, voter files, and field resources behind Steyer, materially improving his conditional general-election path.
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results show the June 2, 2026 primary is underway in the canvass period and only the top two will advance, creating a hard gate before November. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor
Recent result coverage consistently placed Steyer third behind Hilton and Becerra as counting continued. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

KTLA reported Hilton around 28%, Becerra around 26%, and Steyer around 20% with roughly 56% counted, leaving Steyer materially behind for a top-two place. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

Friday-update reporting said there were no major shifts in the governor race as ballots continued to be counted. https://www.desertsun.com/story/news/nation/california/2026/06/04/california-primary-results-latest-governor-los-angeles-mayor-house/90403103007/

The June 1 CEPP poll had Becerra 29%, Hilton 23%, and Steyer 18% after undecideds were pushed, matching the broad pattern that Steyer was competitive but not leading. https://www.cpp.edu/class/political-science/docs/media/cepp_2026_california_primary_poll_results_june_1_2026.pdf

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to make the top two in the June 2, 2026 primary, loses the November 3, 2026 general election, withdraws, or the market resolves to Other because the result is not confirmed by July 31, 2027.
supporting_evidence: California uses a top-two primary, so finishing third is functionally near-elimination because only two candidates advance to November. https://ballotpedia.org/California_gubernatorial_election%2C_2026_%28June_2_top-two_primary%29

Official state results show the primary remains unofficial pending certification, meaning the immediate bottleneck is the canvass rather than the general election. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

Multiple outlets reported Hilton and Becerra holding the top two positions while Steyer trailed in third as counting continued. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

NPR described the contest as Democrats and Republicans vying for the top two, with Steyer one of the candidates in close contention but not in the lead. https://www.npr.org/2026/06/03/nx-s1-5844747/california-primary-election-results-governor

OC Register reported Hilton and Becerra positioned in the top two spots and expressing confidence that their standings would hold. https://www.ocregister.com/2026/06/02/2026-election-results-californias-governor-race-becerra-steyer-hilton-face-off-against-58-others/

A NewsNation/DDHQ result page said Decision Desk HQ projected Becerra would advance, further narrowing Steyer's path. https://www.newsnationnow.com/politics/2026-midterm-elections/california-governor-race-primary-results-2026/
contradicting_evidence: The Secretary of State notes the results are still unofficial and will not be certified until July 10, 2026, so a late-count reversal is still technically possible. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026

California counts ballots slowly after election day, and national/live result pages continued to show the race as not fully called during the canvass. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

California's strong Democratic lean means that if Steyer somehow reaches the general election, he could still be viable in some runoff matchups. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/report-registration/15day-primary-2026

New evidence weakens the NO case at the margin by suggesting that, if Steyer does overtake Becerra in late-certified returns, Becerra would not litigate or demand a recount and major Democratic and union organizations would rapidly unify behind Steyer for November.

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 4%, therefore yes_prob = 4%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The new evidence modestly improves Steyer's conditional path after a top-two breakthrough: if late-certified returns lift him past Becerra, he would face less risk of a recount or court fight from Becerra and would likely inherit a unified Democratic Party and major union field operation against Hilton. That meaningfully strengthens his November viability conditional on advancing. However, the central obstacle remains unchanged: Steyer was still running third in the canvass, so he first needs a late-count reversal just to reach the runoff. As a result, YES rises from a very small chance to a still-small chance, while NO remains strongly favored.
