question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC decision desks
Campaign donors, endorsers, unions, and party networks
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election regardless of party
Steyer must first finish in the top two statewide in the June 2, 2026 primary canvass
If Steyer advances, he must then win the November 3, 2026 gubernatorial general election
The market resolves when AP, Fox News, and NBC all call the race for the same candidate, or else by official certification
If the election is not confirmed by July 31, 2027, the market resolves to Other, which is effectively NO for this binary market
latent_variables: How many uncounted ballots remain and whether their partisan/geographic mix favors Steyer disproportionately
Whether Steyer can overtake Becerra or Hilton during the canvass to claim a top-two spot
General-election strength of Steyer conditional on making the runoff
Late endorsements, legal challenges, recount dynamics, or candidate withdrawals
How California's Democratic lean interacts with the eventual two-candidate runoff matchup

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election, either after advancing from the June 2, 2026 top-two primary and then winning the November 3, 2026 general election, with AP/Fox/NBC all calling the race for him or official certification establishing him as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Steyer is a real, active candidate with an official campaign site stating he is running for Governor of California in 2026. https://www.tomsteyer.com/meet-tom

NBC Los Angeles reported Steyer had spent more than $100 million on the campaign, indicating serious capacity and organization. https://www.nbclosangeles.com/news/local/who-is-tom-steyer-california-governor-race-election/3883007/

Some recent coverage said Steyer was not conceding and still arguing he had a path to the November runoff while ballots were being counted. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/

Pre-primary polls showed Steyer in the top tier, though not clearly ahead; Emerson had him tied for second in one survey. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-continues-to-surge-steyer-and-hilton-compete-for-second-spot/

New evidence indicates California officials opened an expedited audit after a ballot-export formatting error omitted some cross-county vote-center tallies from the statewide unofficial governor dashboard, and two large Southern California counties said tens of thousands of records must be reprocessed; if those metropolitan vote centers skew toward Steyer as his campaign claims, his path to the top two improves modestly.
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results show the June 2, 2026 primary is underway in the canvass period and only the top two will advance, creating a hard gate before November. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor
Recent result coverage consistently placed Steyer third behind Hilton and Becerra as counting continued. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

KTLA reported Hilton around 28%, Becerra around 26%, and Steyer around 20% with roughly 56% counted, leaving Steyer materially behind for a top-two place. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

Friday-update reporting said there were no major shifts in the governor race as ballots continued to be counted. https://www.desertsun.com/story/news/nation/california/2026/06/04/california-primary-results-latest-governor-los-angeles-mayor-house/90403103007/

The June 1 CEPP poll had Becerra 29%, Hilton 23%, and Steyer 18% after undecideds were pushed, matching the broad pattern that Steyer was competitive but not leading. https://www.cpp.edu/class/political-science/docs/media/cepp_2026_california_primary_poll_results_june_1_2026.pdf

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to make the top two in the June 2, 2026 primary, loses the November 3, 2026 general election, withdraws, or the market resolves to Other because the result is not confirmed by July 31, 2027.
supporting_evidence: California uses a top-two primary, so finishing third is functionally near-elimination because only two candidates advance to November. https://ballotpedia.org/California_gubernatorial_election%2C_2026_%28June_2_top-two_primary%29

Official state results show the primary remains unofficial pending certification, meaning the immediate bottleneck is the canvass rather than the general election. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

Multiple outlets reported Hilton and Becerra holding the top two positions while Steyer trailed in third as counting continued. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

NPR described the contest as Democrats and Republicans vying for the top two, with Steyer one of the candidates in close contention but not in the lead. https://www.npr.org/2026/06/03/nx-s1-5844747/california-primary-election-results-governor

OC Register reported Hilton and Becerra positioned in the top two spots and expressing confidence that their standings would hold. https://www.ocregister.com/2026/06/02/2026-election-results-californias-governor-race-becerra-steyer-hilton-face-off-against-58-others/

A NewsNation/DDHQ result page said Decision Desk HQ projected Becerra would advance, further narrowing Steyer's path. https://www.newsnationnow.com/politics/2026-midterm-elections/california-governor-race-primary-results-2026/
contradicting_evidence: The Secretary of State notes the results are still unofficial and will not be certified until July 10, 2026, so a late-count reversal is still technically possible. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026

California counts ballots slowly after election day, and national/live result pages continued to show the race as not fully called during the canvass. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

California's strong Democratic lean means that if Steyer somehow reaches the general election, he could still be viable in some runoff matchups. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/report-registration/15day-primary-2026

New evidence says an expedited audit was opened after a ballot-export formatting error omitted some cross-county vote-center tallies from the statewide unofficial governor dashboard, with tens of thousands of records to be reprocessed in two large Southern California counties; that makes the current third-place standing less reliable than previously assumed.

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 6%, therefore yes_prob = 6%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The new evidence weakens the most important premise behind the original forecast: that the unofficial statewide dashboard accurately reflected the governor standings during the canvass. An acknowledged ballot-export formatting error affecting cross-county vote-center tallies, with tens of thousands of records to be reprocessed in two large Southern California counties, materially increases the chance that Steyer can improve enough to reach the top two. However, this is still only a modest upward revision because the evidence says the underlying cast ballots were not affected, only reporting files, and Steyer was previously described as materially behind both Hilton and Becerra. So his path is now less remote, but still clearly unlikely overall.

question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC decision desks
Campaign donors, endorsers, unions, and party networks
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election regardless of party
Steyer must first finish in the top two statewide in the June 2, 2026 primary canvass
If Steyer advances, he must then win the November 3, 2026 general election
The market resolves when AP, Fox News, and NBC all call the race for the same candidate, or else by official certification
If the election is not confirmed by July 31, 2027, the market resolves to Other, which is effectively NO for this binary market
latent_variables: How many uncounted ballots remain and whether their partisan/geographic mix favors Steyer disproportionately
Whether Steyer can overtake Becerra or Hilton during the canvass to claim a top-two spot
General-election strength of Steyer conditional on making the runoff
Late endorsements, legal challenges, recount dynamics, or candidate withdrawals
How California's Democratic lean interacts with the eventual two-candidate runoff matchup

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election, either after advancing from the June 2, 2026 top-two primary and then winning the November 3, 2026 general election, with AP/Fox/NBC all calling the race for him or official certification establishing him as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Steyer is a real, active candidate with an official campaign site stating he is running for Governor of California in 2026. https://www.tomsteyer.com/meet-tom

NBC Los Angeles reported Steyer had spent more than $100 million on the campaign, indicating serious capacity and organization. https://www.nbclosangeles.com/news/local/who-is-tom-steyer-california-governor-race-election/3883007/

Some recent coverage said Steyer was not conceding and still arguing he had a path to the November runoff while ballots were being counted. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/

Pre-primary polls showed Steyer in the top tier, though not clearly ahead; Emerson had him tied for second in one survey. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-continues-to-surge-steyer-and-hilton-compete-for-second-spot/
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results show the June 2, 2026 primary is underway in the canvass period and only the top two will advance, creating a hard gate before November. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor
Recent result coverage consistently placed Steyer third behind Hilton and Becerra as counting continued. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

KTLA reported Hilton around 28%, Becerra around 26%, and Steyer around 20% with roughly 56% counted, leaving Steyer materially behind for a top-two place. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

Friday-update reporting said there were no major shifts in the governor race as ballots continued to be counted. https://www.desertsun.com/story/news/nation/california/2026/06/04/california-primary-results-latest-governor-los-angeles-mayor-house/90403103007/

The June 1 CEPP poll had Becerra 29%, Hilton 23%, and Steyer 18% after undecideds were pushed, matching the broad pattern that Steyer was competitive but not leading. https://www.cpp.edu/class/political-science/docs/media/cepp_2026_california_primary_poll_results_june_1_2026.pdf

New post-election tabulation evidence said about 1.1 million ballots remained, more than 70% from Los Angeles and Bay Area counties where Becerra was already outperforming Steyer; Steyer was estimated to need about a 9-point win in the outstanding vote to pass Becerra, but late-counted batches had instead favored Becerra by about 6 points.

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to make the top two in the June 2, 2026 primary, loses the November 3, 2026 general election, withdraws, or the market resolves to Other because the result is not confirmed by July 31, 2027.
supporting_evidence: California uses a top-two primary, so finishing third is functionally near-elimination because only two candidates advance to November. https://ballotpedia.org/California_gubernatorial_election%2C_2026_%28June_2_top-two_primary%29

Official state results show the primary remains unofficial pending certification, meaning the immediate bottleneck is the canvass rather than the general election. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

Multiple outlets reported Hilton and Becerra holding the top two positions while Steyer trailed in third as counting continued. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

NPR described the contest as Democrats and Republicans vying for the top two, with Steyer one of the candidates in close contention but not in the lead. https://www.npr.org/2026/06/03/nx-s1-5844747/california-primary-election-results-governor

OC Register reported Hilton and Becerra positioned in the top two spots and expressing confidence that their standings would hold. https://www.ocregister.com/2026/06/02/2026-election-results-californias-governor-race-becerra-steyer-hilton-face-off-against-58-others/

A NewsNation/DDHQ result page said Decision Desk HQ projected Becerra would advance, further narrowing Steyer's path. https://www.newsnationnow.com/politics/2026-midterm-elections/california-governor-race-primary-results-2026/

New post-election tabulation evidence estimated about 1.1 million ballots remained, concentrated in counties where Becerra was already running well ahead of Steyer; Steyer was estimated to need roughly a 9-point margin in the outstanding vote to pass Becerra, but recent late ballots had favored Becerra by about 6 points, implying the remaining pool was not breaking in Steyer's favor.
contradicting_evidence: The Secretary of State notes the results are still unofficial and will not be certified until July 10, 2026, so a late-count reversal is still technically possible. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026

California counts ballots slowly after election day, and national/live result pages continued to show the race as not fully called during the canvass. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

California's strong Democratic lean means that if Steyer somehow reaches the general election, he could still be viable in some runoff matchups. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/report-registration/15day-primary-2026

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 0.5%, therefore yes_prob = 0.5%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The new evidence sharply weakens Steyer's already narrow path. Previously, the residual uncertainty came from the possibility that uncounted California ballots might lean toward him enough to erase his deficit for second place. The update instead says the remaining ballot pool is concentrated in regions where Becerra is already stronger, that Steyer would need an implausibly large roughly 9-point advantage in the outstanding vote to catch Becerra, and that late-counted batches have actually favored Becerra by about 6 points. That makes a Steyer runoff berth markedly less likely, and because he cannot win the governorship without first making the top two, the market is now even more likely to resolve NO.

question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC decision desks
Campaign donors, endorsers, unions, and party networks
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election regardless of party
Steyer must first finish in the top two statewide in the June 2, 2026 primary canvass
If Steyer advances, he must then win the November 3, 2026 gubernatorial general election
The market resolves when AP, Fox News, and NBC all call the race for the same candidate, or else by official certification
If the election is not confirmed by July 31, 2027, the market resolves to Other, which is effectively NO for this binary market
latent_variables: How many uncounted ballots remain and whether their partisan/geographic mix favors Steyer disproportionately
Whether Steyer can overtake Becerra or Hilton during the canvass to claim a top-two spot
General-election strength of Steyer conditional on making the runoff
Late endorsements, legal challenges, recount dynamics, or candidate withdrawals
How California's Democratic lean interacts with the eventual two-candidate runoff matchup

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election, either after advancing from the June 2, 2026 top-two primary and then winning the November 3, 2026 general election, with AP/Fox/NBC all calling the race for him or official certification establishing him as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Steyer is a real, active candidate with an official campaign site stating he is running for Governor of California in 2026. https://www.tomsteyer.com/meet-tom

NBC Los Angeles reported Steyer had spent more than $100 million on the campaign, indicating serious capacity and organization. https://www.nbclosangeles.com/news/local/who-is-tom-steyer-california-governor-race-election/3883007/

Some recent coverage said Steyer was not conceding and still arguing he had a path to the November runoff while ballots were being counted. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/

Pre-primary polls showed Steyer in the top tier, though not clearly ahead; Emerson had him tied for second in one survey. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-continues-to-surge-steyer-and-hilton-compete-for-second-spot/
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results show the June 2, 2026 primary is underway in the canvass period and only the top two will advance, creating a hard gate before November. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor
Recent result coverage consistently placed Steyer third behind Hilton and Becerra as counting continued. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

KTLA reported Hilton around 28%, Becerra around 26%, and Steyer around 20% with roughly 56% counted, leaving Steyer materially behind for a top-two place. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

Friday-update reporting said there were no major shifts in the governor race as ballots continued to be counted. https://www.desertsun.com/story/news/nation/california/2026/06/04/california-primary-results-latest-governor-los-angeles-mayor-house/90403103007/

The June 1 CEPP poll had Becerra 29%, Hilton 23%, and Steyer 18% after undecideds were pushed, matching the broad pattern that Steyer was competitive but not leading. https://www.cpp.edu/class/political-science/docs/media/cepp_2026_california_primary_poll_results_june_1_2026.pdf

New evidence: on June 6 Steyer said he would not request a recount or file any court challenge, and would respect the canvass, which sharply reduces the already-small chance that a post-election process could move him into the top two.

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to make the top two in the June 2, 2026 primary, loses the November 3, 2026 general election, withdraws, or the market resolves to Other because the result is not confirmed by July 31, 2027.
supporting_evidence: California uses a top-two primary, so finishing third is functionally near-elimination because only two candidates advance to November. https://ballotpedia.org/California_gubernatorial_election%2C_2026_%28June_2_top-two_primary%29

Official state results show the primary remains unofficial pending certification, meaning the immediate bottleneck is the canvass rather than the general election. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

Multiple outlets reported Hilton and Becerra holding the top two positions while Steyer trailed in third as counting continued. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

NPR described the contest as Democrats and Republicans vying for the top two, with Steyer one of the candidates in close contention but not in the lead. https://www.npr.org/2026/06/03/nx-s1-5844747/california-primary-election-results-governor

OC Register reported Hilton and Becerra positioned in the top two spots and expressing confidence that their standings would hold. https://www.ocregister.com/2026/06/02/2026-election-results-californias-governor-race-becerra-steyer-hilton-face-off-against-58-others/

A NewsNation/DDHQ result page said Decision Desk HQ projected Becerra would advance, further narrowing Steyer's path. https://www.newsnationnow.com/politics/2026-midterm-elections/california-governor-race-primary-results-2026/

New evidence: on June 6 Steyer said he would not request a recount or file any court challenge and acknowledged no realistic path into the top two, which materially lowers the chance of any reversal before certification.
contradicting_evidence: The Secretary of State notes the results are still unofficial and will not be certified until July 10, 2026, so a late-count reversal is still technically possible. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026

California counts ballots slowly after election day, and national/live result pages continued to show the race as not fully called during the canvass. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

California's strong Democratic lean means that if Steyer somehow reaches the general election, he could still be viable in some runoff matchups. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/report-registration/15day-primary-2026

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 0.5%, therefore yes_prob = 0.5%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: What changed is not the vote count itself but the remaining paths by which Steyer could still reach the top two. In the initial forecast, a small residual YES probability remained because California was still canvassing ballots and a recount, legal challenge, or other post-election process could in principle alter the ordering. The new evidence says Steyer now concedes he has no realistic path, will not seek a recount, and will not file a court challenge. That removes much of the already-thin upside tail. A tiny nonzero probability remains because the canvass is still unofficial and extraordinary counting outcomes are still logically possible, but the chance of YES is now even smaller.

question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC decision desks
Campaign donors, endorsers, unions, and party networks
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election regardless of party
Steyer must first finish in the top two statewide in the June 2, 2026 primary canvass
If Steyer advances, he must then win the November 3, 2026 general election
The market resolves when AP, Fox News, and NBC all call the race for the same candidate, or else by official certification
If the election is not confirmed by July 31, 2027, the market resolves to Other, which is effectively NO for this binary market
latent_variables: How many uncounted ballots remain and whether their partisan/geographic mix favors Steyer disproportionately
Whether Steyer can overtake Becerra or Hilton during the canvass to claim a top-two spot
General-election strength of Steyer conditional on making the runoff
Late endorsements, legal challenges, recount dynamics, or candidate withdrawals
How California's Democratic lean interacts with the eventual two-candidate runoff matchup

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election, either after advancing from the June 2, 2026 top-two primary and then winning the November 3, 2026 general election, with AP/Fox/NBC all calling the race for him or official certification establishing him as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Steyer is a real, active candidate with an official campaign site stating he is running for Governor of California in 2026. https://www.tomsteyer.com/meet-tom

NBC Los Angeles reported Steyer had spent more than $100 million on the campaign, indicating serious capacity and organization. https://www.nbclosangeles.com/news/local/who-is-tom-steyer-california-governor-race-election/3883007/

Some recent coverage said Steyer was not conceding and still arguing he had a path to the November runoff while ballots were being counted. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/

Pre-primary polls showed Steyer in the top tier, though not clearly ahead; Emerson had him tied for second in one survey. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-continues-to-surge-steyer-and-hilton-compete-for-second-spot/
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results show the June 2, 2026 primary is underway in the canvass period and only the top two will advance, creating a hard gate before November. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor
Recent result coverage consistently placed Steyer third behind Hilton and Becerra as counting continued. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

KTLA reported Hilton around 28%, Becerra around 26%, and Steyer around 20% with roughly 56% counted, leaving Steyer materially behind for a top-two place. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

Friday-update reporting said there were no major shifts in the governor race as ballots continued to be counted. https://www.desertsun.com/story/news/nation/california/2026/06/04/california-primary-results-latest-governor-los-angeles-mayor-house/90403103007/

The June 1 CEPP poll had Becerra 29%, Hilton 23%, and Steyer 18% after undecideds were pushed, matching the broad pattern that Steyer was competitive but not leading. https://www.cpp.edu/class/political-science/docs/media/cepp_2026_california_primary_poll_results_june_1_2026.pdf

New evidence indicates county deadlines to cure signature mismatches and missing ID on provisional and mail ballots have expired, with most remaining work now limited to tabulating already-accepted ballots rather than restoring rejected ones, sharply reducing the pool of ballots through which Steyer could plausibly make up ground.

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to make the top two in the June 2, 2026 primary, loses the November 3, 2026 general election, withdraws, or the market resolves to Other because the result is not confirmed by July 31, 2027.
supporting_evidence: California uses a top-two primary, so finishing third is functionally near-elimination because only two candidates advance to November. https://ballotpedia.org/California_gubernatorial_election%2C_2026_%28June_2_top-two_primary%29

Official state results show the primary remains unofficial pending certification, meaning the immediate bottleneck is the canvass rather than the general election. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

Multiple outlets reported Hilton and Becerra holding the top two positions while Steyer trailed in third as counting continued. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

NPR described the contest as Democrats and Republicans vying for the top two, with Steyer one of the candidates in close contention but not in the lead. https://www.npr.org/2026/06/03/nx-s1-5844747/california-primary-election-results-governor

OC Register reported Hilton and Becerra positioned in the top two spots and expressing confidence that their standings would hold. https://www.ocregister.com/2026/06/02/2026-election-results-californias-governor-race-becerra-steyer-hilton-face-off-against-58-others/

A NewsNation/DDHQ result page said Decision Desk HQ projected Becerra would advance, further narrowing Steyer's path. https://www.newsnationnow.com/politics/2026-midterm-elections/california-governor-race-primary-results-2026/

New evidence indicates ballot-cure deadlines have expired in key counties and that most remaining canvass work is administrative tabulation of already-accepted ballots, not recovery of rejected ballots, which further reduces the odds of any late-count rescue for Steyer.
contradicting_evidence: The Secretary of State notes the results are still unofficial and will not be certified until July 10, 2026, so a late-count reversal is still technically possible. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026

California counts ballots slowly after election day, and national/live result pages continued to show the race as not fully called during the canvass. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

California's strong Democratic lean means that if Steyer somehow reaches the general election, he could still be viable in some runoff matchups. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/report-registration/15day-primary-2026

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 1%, therefore yes_prob = 1%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The key update is that the window for curing signature mismatches and missing-ID ballot problems has expired in major counties, and election officials say most remaining canvass activity is now administrative tabulation of already-accepted ballots rather than recovery of rejected ballots. That meaningfully shrinks the already-small set of mechanisms by which Steyer could gain enough votes late to move from third into the top two. Because Steyer was already trailing materially, this new evidence pushes the chance of a runoff comeback lower still, making NO even more likely.

question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC decision desks
Campaign donors, endorsers, unions, and party networks
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election regardless of party
Steyer must first finish in the top two statewide in the June 2, 2026 primary canvass
If Steyer advances, he must then win the November 3, 2026 general election
The market resolves when AP, Fox News, and NBC all call the race for the same candidate, or else by official certification
If the election is not confirmed by July 31, 2027, the market resolves to Other, which is effectively NO for this binary market
latent_variables: How many uncounted ballots remain and whether their partisan/geographic mix favors Steyer disproportionately
Whether Steyer can overtake Becerra or Hilton during the canvass to claim a top-two spot
General-election strength of Steyer conditional on making the runoff
Late endorsements, legal challenges, recount dynamics, or candidate withdrawals
How California's Democratic lean interacts with the eventual two-candidate runoff matchup

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election, either after advancing from the June 2, 2026 top-two primary and then winning the November 3, 2026 general election, with AP/Fox/NBC all calling the race for him or official certification establishing him as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Steyer is a real, active candidate with an official campaign site stating he is running for Governor of California in 2026. https://www.tomsteyer.com/meet-tom

NBC Los Angeles reported Steyer had spent more than $100 million on the campaign, indicating serious capacity and organization. https://www.nbclosangeles.com/news/local/who-is-tom-steyer-california-governor-race-election/3883007/

Some recent coverage said Steyer was not conceding and still arguing he had a path to the November runoff while ballots were being counted. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/

Pre-primary polls showed Steyer in the top tier, though not clearly ahead; Emerson had him tied for second in one survey. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-continues-to-surge-steyer-and-hilton-compete-for-second-spot/
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results show the June 2, 2026 primary is underway in the canvass period and only the top two will advance, creating a hard gate before November. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor
Recent result coverage consistently placed Steyer third behind Hilton and Becerra as counting continued. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

KTLA reported Hilton around 28%, Becerra around 26%, and Steyer around 20% with roughly 56% counted, leaving Steyer materially behind for a top-two place. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

Friday-update reporting said there were no major shifts in the governor race as ballots continued to be counted. https://www.desertsun.com/story/news/nation/california/2026/06/04/california-primary-results-latest-governor-los-angeles-mayor-house/90403103007/

The June 1 CEPP poll had Becerra 29%, Hilton 23%, and Steyer 18% after undecideds were pushed, matching the broad pattern that Steyer was competitive but not leading. https://www.cpp.edu/class/political-science/docs/media/cepp_2026_california_primary_poll_results_june_1_2026.pdf

New evidence says the Secretary of State is only delaying nightly posting of county statement-of-vote PDFs for website maintenance while counties continue canvassing normally, which does not create a substantive new path for Steyer to gain ground.

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to make the top two in the June 2, 2026 primary, loses the November 3, 2026 general election, withdraws, or the market resolves to Other because the result is not confirmed by July 31, 2027.
supporting_evidence: California uses a top-two primary, so finishing third is functionally near-elimination because only two candidates advance to November. https://ballotpedia.org/California_gubernatorial_election%2C_2026_%28June_2_top-two_primary%29

Official state results show the primary remains unofficial pending certification, meaning the immediate bottleneck is the canvass rather than the general election. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

Multiple outlets reported Hilton and Becerra holding the top two positions while Steyer trailed in third as counting continued. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

NPR described the contest as Democrats and Republicans vying for the top two, with Steyer one of the candidates in close contention but not in the lead. https://www.npr.org/2026/06/03/nx-s1-5844747/california-primary-election-results-governor

OC Register reported Hilton and Becerra positioned in the top two spots and expressing confidence that their standings would hold. https://www.ocregister.com/2026/06/02/2026-election-results-californias-governor-race-becerra-steyer-hilton-face-off-against-58-others/

A NewsNation/DDHQ result page said Decision Desk HQ projected Becerra would advance, further narrowing Steyer's path. https://www.newsnationnow.com/politics/2026-midterm-elections/california-governor-race-primary-results-2026/

New evidence says the Secretary of State's temporary shift in posting county statement-of-vote PDFs is only a public-website timing change during maintenance, while counties keep canvassing on the normal timetable, so the underlying count process remains unchanged.
contradicting_evidence: The Secretary of State notes the results are still unofficial and will not be certified until July 10, 2026, so a late-count reversal is still technically possible. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026

California counts ballots slowly after election day, and national/live result pages continued to show the race as not fully called during the canvass. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

California's strong Democratic lean means that if Steyer somehow reaches the general election, he could still be viable in some runoff matchups. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/report-registration/15day-primary-2026

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 2%, therefore yes_prob = 2%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The new evidence is operational rather than electoral: it says only that the public posting time for county statement-of-vote PDFs will temporarily move from 8 p.m. to 10 p.m. during maintenance, while counties continue canvassing under the normal timetable. That slightly affects public visibility into supporting documents but does not materially change the count, the certification path, or Steyer's need to overcome an existing third-place position to reach the top two. So the forecast remains effectively unchanged.

question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC decision desks
Campaign donors, endorsers, unions, and party networks
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election regardless of party
Steyer must first finish in the top two statewide in the June 2, 2026 primary canvass
If Steyer advances, he must then win the November 3, 2026 gubernatorial general election
The market resolves when AP, Fox News, and NBC all call the race for the same candidate, or else by official certification
If the election is not confirmed by July 31, 2027, the market resolves to Other, which is effectively NO for this binary market
latent_variables: How many uncounted ballots remain and whether their partisan/geographic mix favors Steyer disproportionately
Whether Steyer can overtake Becerra or Hilton during the canvass to claim a top-two spot
General-election strength of Steyer conditional on making the runoff
Late endorsements, legal challenges, recount dynamics, or candidate withdrawals
How California's Democratic lean interacts with the eventual two-candidate runoff matchup

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election, either after advancing from the June 2, 2026 top-two primary and then winning the November 3, 2026 general election, with AP/Fox/NBC all calling the race for him or official certification establishing him as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Steyer is a real, active candidate with an official campaign site stating he is running for Governor of California in 2026. https://www.tomsteyer.com/meet-tom

NBC Los Angeles reported Steyer had spent more than $100 million on the campaign, indicating serious capacity and organization. https://www.nbclosangeles.com/news/local/who-is-tom-steyer-california-governor-race-election/3883007/

Some recent coverage said Steyer was not conceding and still arguing he had a path to the November runoff while ballots were being counted. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/

Pre-primary polls showed Steyer in the top tier, though not clearly ahead; Emerson had him tied for second in one survey. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-continues-to-surge-steyer-and-hilton-compete-for-second-spot/
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results show the June 2, 2026 primary is underway in the canvass period and only the top two will advance, creating a hard gate before November. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor
Recent result coverage consistently placed Steyer third behind Hilton and Becerra as counting continued. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

KTLA reported Hilton around 28%, Becerra around 26%, and Steyer around 20% with roughly 56% counted, leaving Steyer materially behind for a top-two place. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

Friday-update reporting said there were no major shifts in the governor race as ballots continued to be counted. https://www.desertsun.com/story/news/nation/california/2026/06/04/california-primary-results-latest-governor-los-angeles-mayor-house/90403103007/

The June 1 CEPP poll had Becerra 29%, Hilton 23%, and Steyer 18% after undecideds were pushed, matching the broad pattern that Steyer was competitive but not leading. https://www.cpp.edu/class/political-science/docs/media/cepp_2026_california_primary_poll_results_june_1_2026.pdf

New evidence about NBC appointing Marisa Chen to oversee West Coast decision-desk operations explicitly says the reorganization did not alter NBC's criteria for calling elections, so it does not create a new procedural path that would improve Steyer's chances.

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to make the top two in the June 2, 2026 primary, loses the November 3, 2026 general election, withdraws, or the market resolves to Other because the result is not confirmed by July 31, 2027.
supporting_evidence: California uses a top-two primary, so finishing third is functionally near-elimination because only two candidates advance to November. https://ballotpedia.org/California_gubernatorial_election%2C_2026_%28June_2_top-two_primary%29

Official state results show the primary remains unofficial pending certification, meaning the immediate bottleneck is the canvass rather than the general election. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

Multiple outlets reported Hilton and Becerra holding the top two positions while Steyer trailed in third as counting continued. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

NPR described the contest as Democrats and Republicans vying for the top two, with Steyer one of the candidates in close contention but not in the lead. https://www.npr.org/2026/06/03/nx-s1-5844747/california-primary-election-results-governor

OC Register reported Hilton and Becerra positioned in the top two spots and expressing confidence that their standings would hold. https://www.ocregister.com/2026/06/02/2026-election-results-californias-governor-race-becerra-steyer-hilton-face-off-against-58-others/

A NewsNation/DDHQ result page said Decision Desk HQ projected Becerra would advance, further narrowing Steyer's path. https://www.newsnationnow.com/politics/2026-midterm-elections/california-governor-race-primary-results-2026/

New evidence about NBC appointing Marisa Chen to oversee West Coast decision-desk operations explicitly says the reorganization did not alter NBC's criteria for calling elections, implying no meaningful change to the market's resolution mechanics or to Steyer's already-narrow path.
contradicting_evidence: The Secretary of State notes the results are still unofficial and will not be certified until July 10, 2026, so a late-count reversal is still technically possible. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026

California counts ballots slowly after election day, and national/live result pages continued to show the race as not fully called during the canvass. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

California's strong Democratic lean means that if Steyer somehow reaches the general election, he could still be viable in some runoff matchups. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/report-registration/15day-primary-2026

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 2%, therefore yes_prob = 2%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The new evidence is about NBC's internal decision-desk staffing, not vote totals, ballot-count trends, or candidate strength. Because NBC explicitly said the reorganization does not change its election-call criteria, the update is essentially resolution-mechanics neutral. Steyer still appears to need a substantial late-count comeback just to make the top two, so the forecast remains effectively unchanged at a very low YES probability.

question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC decision desks
Campaign donors, endorsers, unions, and party networks
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election regardless of party
Steyer must first finish in the top two statewide in the June 2, 2026 primary canvass
If Steyer advances, he must then win the November 3, 2026 gubernatorial general election
The market resolves when AP, Fox News, and NBC all call the race for the same candidate, or else by official certification
If the election is not confirmed by July 31, 2027, the market resolves to Other, which is effectively NO for this binary market
latent_variables: How many uncounted ballots remain and whether their partisan/geographic mix favors Steyer disproportionately
Whether Steyer can overtake Becerra or Hilton during the canvass to claim a top-two spot
General-election strength of Steyer conditional on making the runoff
Late endorsements, legal challenges, recount dynamics, or candidate withdrawals
How California's Democratic lean interacts with the eventual two-candidate runoff matchup

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election, either after advancing from the June 2, 2026 top-two primary and then winning the November 3, 2026 general election, with AP/Fox/NBC all calling the race for him or official certification establishing him as the winner.
supporting_evidence: Steyer is a real, active candidate with an official campaign site stating he is running for Governor of California in 2026. https://www.tomsteyer.com/meet-tom

NBC Los Angeles reported Steyer had spent more than $100 million on the campaign, indicating serious capacity and organization. https://www.nbclosangeles.com/news/local/who-is-tom-steyer-california-governor-race-election/3883007/

Some recent coverage said Steyer was not conceding and still arguing he had a path to the November runoff while ballots were being counted. https://www.dailynews.com/2026/06/04/california-governors-race-does-tom-steyer-still-have-a-path-to-the-november-runoff/

Pre-primary polls showed Steyer in the top tier, though not clearly ahead; Emerson had him tied for second in one survey. https://emersoncollegepolling.com/california-2026-poll-becerra-continues-to-surge-steyer-and-hilton-compete-for-second-spot/

New evidence: routine Sacramento bookings by political committees, advocacy groups, and media crews are consistent with a normal transition toward the November race, so Steyer's path remains technically alive if he reaches the runoff, though the operators explicitly said the activity does not indicate which candidate is likely to advance or win.
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State results show the June 2, 2026 primary is underway in the canvass period and only the top two will advance, creating a hard gate before November. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor
Recent result coverage consistently placed Steyer third behind Hilton and Becerra as counting continued. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

KTLA reported Hilton around 28%, Becerra around 26%, and Steyer around 20% with roughly 56% counted, leaving Steyer materially behind for a top-two place. https://ktla.com/news/politics/california-governor-race-hilton-becerra/

Friday-update reporting said there were no major shifts in the governor race as ballots continued to be counted. https://www.desertsun.com/story/news/nation/california/2026/06/04/california-primary-results-latest-governor-los-angeles-mayor-house/90403103007/

The June 1 CEPP poll had Becerra 29%, Hilton 23%, and Steyer 18% after undecideds were pushed, matching the broad pattern that Steyer was competitive but not leading. https://www.cpp.edu/class/political-science/docs/media/cepp_2026_california_primary_poll_results_june_1_2026.pdf

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, including if he fails to make the top two in the June 2, 2026 primary, loses the November 3, 2026 general election, withdraws, or the market resolves to Other because the result is not confirmed by July 31, 2027.
supporting_evidence: California uses a top-two primary, so finishing third is functionally near-elimination because only two candidates advance to November. https://ballotpedia.org/California_gubernatorial_election%2C_2026_%28June_2_top-two_primary%29

Official state results show the primary remains unofficial pending certification, meaning the immediate bottleneck is the canvass rather than the general election. https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

Multiple outlets reported Hilton and Becerra holding the top two positions while Steyer trailed in third as counting continued. https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/california-governors-race-candidates-2026-primary/

NPR described the contest as Democrats and Republicans vying for the top two, with Steyer one of the candidates in close contention but not in the lead. https://www.npr.org/2026/06/03/nx-s1-5844747/california-primary-election-results-governor

OC Register reported Hilton and Becerra positioned in the top two spots and expressing confidence that their standings would hold. https://www.ocregister.com/2026/06/02/2026-election-results-californias-governor-race-becerra-steyer-hilton-face-off-against-58-others/

A NewsNation/DDHQ result page said Decision Desk HQ projected Becerra would advance, further narrowing Steyer's path. https://www.newsnationnow.com/politics/2026-midterm-elections/california-governor-race-primary-results-2026/

New evidence: Sacramento-area campaign and media bookings were described as routine election-cycle planning and explicitly not as a signal about which candidates are most likely to advance or win, so this does not materially weaken the existing case that Steyer is probably eliminated by his third-place position.
contradicting_evidence: The Secretary of State notes the results are still unofficial and will not be certified until July 10, 2026, so a late-count reversal is still technically possible. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/upcoming-elections/primary-election-june-2-2026

California counts ballots slowly after election day, and national/live result pages continued to show the race as not fully called during the canvass. https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

California's strong Democratic lean means that if Steyer somehow reaches the general election, he could still be viable in some runoff matchups. https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/report-registration/15day-primary-2026

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 2%, therefore yes_prob = 2%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The new evidence is essentially neutral. Routine Sacramento bookings by campaigns, advocacy groups, vendors, and media indicate normal preparation for an election-season fall campaign environment, but the source explicitly says those reservations do not imply anything about which candidates are most likely to advance or win. Because the core bottleneck remains unchanged—Steyer was still reported in third place in a top-two primary—the probability that he ultimately wins the governorship is effectively unchanged.

question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?

key_actors: California Secretary of State and county election officials
key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election
Steyer must first overcome his current third-place position in the ongoing primary count to qualify for the general election
If Steyer advances, he must then win the general election and be recognized as the winner by AP, Fox News, and NBC, or by official certification
Late-counted vote-by-mail, provisional, and other ballots can still shift the primary standings before certification
latent_variables: How the remaining unprocessed ballots are distributed between Steyer and Becerra
Whether any late-count geographic pattern disproportionately favors Steyer
Whether media decision desks eventually project a different top-two outcome before certification
If Steyer were to advance, the strength of his general-election matchup and coalition

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election, meaning he first advances from the top-two primary and then is ultimately called or certified as the winner of the November 3, 2026 general election.
supporting_evidence: Tom Steyer is an actual declared candidate for California governor in 2026 on his campaign website: https://www.tomsteyer.com/

Major news coverage reported that Steyer entered the 2026 California governor's race, confirming a real candidacy rather than a hypothetical one: https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-election/climate-activist-billionaire-tom-steyer-run-california-governor-rcna244783
California still had substantial ballot counting remaining after Election Day, leaving some nonzero chance of a late-count reversal: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/unprocessed-ballots-status
AP had not yet called the primary top-two outcome at the time reviewed, implying residual uncertainty remained: https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

Pre-election polling showed Steyer was competitive for a top-two slot, even if not leading: https://www.nbclosangeles.com/news/local/california-governor-poll-xavier-becerra-steve-hilton-tom-steyer/3897267/

New evidence says late-counted vote-by-mail ballots have broken sharply toward Steyer, cutting his statewide deficit for second place to about 0.4 percentage points, or roughly 28,000 votes, which materially improves his chance to reach the runoff.
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State statewide results reviewed on June 5, 2026 showed Steyer in third place at 20.2%, behind Xavier Becerra at 26.0% and Steve Hilton at 27.2%: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor
Steyer trailed Becerra by 330,495 votes in the official statewide count reviewed, making advancement to the general election unlikely: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

NBC's live results also showed Hilton first, Becerra second, and Steyer third, reinforcing the official statewide picture: https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

NBC's post-election coverage described California's governor race as unsettled but still had Hilton, Becerra, and Steyer in that order, with no sign of Steyer overtaking Becerra: https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-election/california-governor-key-races-house-unsettled-vote-count-continues-rcna348210

County-level AP snapshots did not show a clear late-count reservoir large enough to erase Steyer's large statewide deficit to Becerra: https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/
Even after the new update, Steyer remains in third place unofficially, still behind Becerra for the second runoff slot and still needing both a primary comeback and then a general-election win.

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, whether because he fails to advance from the top-two primary, loses the general election, withdraws, or otherwise is not the ultimately recognized winner.
supporting_evidence: California's official statewide results page showed Steyer in third place rather than in a top-two qualifying position: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor
Under California's top-two system, only the top two candidates advance, so Steyer's current third-place position is the dominant reason NO is favored: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/

Steyer's deficit to Becerra was very large relative to the remaining ballots, implying he would need an unusually favorable late-count break to advance: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

NBC's live results and analysis both continued to show Steyer behind Becerra after additional counting, indicating no visible breakout in the past 48 hours reviewed: https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

AP's live results likewise showed Hilton and Becerra ahead of Steyer and had not indicated a Steyer path into the top two: https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/
The new evidence improves Steyer's standing substantially, but he is still unofficially about 28,000 votes short of second place and has not yet secured a runoff berth.
contradicting_evidence: Millions of ballots remained unprocessed, so the primary outcome was not yet certified and a comeback was still mathematically possible: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/unprocessed-ballots-status
AP had not formally called which two candidates would advance at the time reviewed, preserving some uncertainty: https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

key_mechanisms: California's top-two primary determines which two candidates advance to the November 3, 2026 general election
Steyer must first overcome his current third-place position in the ongoing primary count to qualify for the general election
If Steyer advances, he must then win the general election and be recognized as the winner by AP, Fox News, and NBC, or by official certification
Late-counted vote-by-mail, provisional, and other ballots can still shift the primary standings before certification
latent_variables: How the remaining unprocessed ballots are distributed between Steyer and Becerra
Whether any late-count geographic pattern disproportionately favors Steyer
Whether media decision desks eventually project a different top-two outcome before certification
If Steyer were to advance, the strength of his general-election matchup and coalition

Major news coverage reported that Steyer entered the 2026 California governor's race, confirming a real candidacy rather than a hypothetical one: https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-election/climate-activist-billionaire-tom-steyer-run-california-governor-rcna244783
California still had substantial ballot counting remaining after Election Day, leaving some nonzero chance of a late-count reversal: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/unprocessed-ballots-status

New evidence indicates that if late-certified returns lift Steyer into the top two, Becerra would not seek a recount or court challenge and California Democratic leaders and major unions would rapidly consolidate resources behind Steyer, improving his general-election path conditional on advancement.

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, whether because he fails to advance from the top-two primary, loses the general election, withdraws, or otherwise is not the ultimately recognized winner.
supporting_evidence: California's official statewide results page showed Steyer in third place rather than in a top-two qualifying position: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor
Under California's top-two system, only the top two candidates advance, so Steyer's current third-place position is the dominant reason NO is favored: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/

Steyer's deficit to Becerra was very large relative to the remaining ballots, implying he would need an unusually favorable late-count break to advance: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

NBC's live results and analysis both continued to show Steyer behind Becerra after additional counting, indicating no visible breakout in the past 48 hours reviewed: https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

AP's live results likewise showed Hilton and Becerra ahead of Steyer and had not indicated a Steyer path into the top two: https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/
contradicting_evidence: Millions of ballots remained unprocessed, so the primary outcome was not yet certified and a comeback was still mathematically possible: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/unprocessed-ballots-status
AP had not formally called which two candidates would advance at the time reviewed, preserving some uncertainty: https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

Steyer had substantial resources and a real statewide campaign, which kept a small residual chance alive despite his current position: https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-election/tom-steyer-unexpected-alliance-progressives-california-governor-race-rcna342878

Pre-election polling suggested Steyer was at least competitive for a runoff spot before votes were fully counted: https://www.nbclosangeles.com/news/local/california-governor-poll-xavier-becerra-steve-hilton-tom-steyer/3897267/

New evidence suggests that if Steyer does sneak into the runoff on late-certified returns, he would face less intra-party resistance and would quickly inherit unified Democratic organizational support, slightly weakening the NO case conditional on primary advancement.

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 2%, therefore yes_prob = 2%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The new evidence modestly increases Steyer's chances because it improves the second-stage path: if late-certified returns lift him into the top two, Becerra would not contest the result and key Democratic institutions would immediately unify behind Steyer against Hilton. That makes a Steyer general-election win more plausible conditional on advancement. But the dominant obstacle remains unchanged: Steyer was still running third and needed a large late-count reversal just to reach the November ballot, so YES rises only slightly from a very low base.

hypotheses.description: The market resolves YES: Tom Steyer wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election, meaning he first advances from the top-two primary and then is ultimately called or certified as the winner of the November 3, 2026 general election.
supporting_evidence: Tom Steyer is an actual declared candidate for California governor in 2026 on his campaign website: https://www.tomsteyer.com/

Major news coverage reported that Steyer entered the 2026 California governor's race, confirming a real candidacy rather than a hypothetical one: https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-election/climate-activist-billionaire-tom-steyer-run-california-governor-rcna244783
California still had substantial ballot counting remaining after Election Day, leaving some nonzero chance of a late-count reversal: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/unprocessed-ballots-status
AP had not yet called the primary top-two outcome at the time reviewed, implying residual uncertainty remained: https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

Pre-election polling showed Steyer was competitive for a top-two slot, even if not leading: https://www.nbclosangeles.com/news/local/california-governor-poll-xavier-becerra-steve-hilton-tom-steyer/3897267/

New evidence says California officials opened an expedited audit after a ballot-export formatting error omitted some cross-county vote-center tallies from the statewide unofficial governor dashboard, creating a more concrete path for standings to move during canvass.
contradicting_evidence: Official California Secretary of State statewide results reviewed on June 5, 2026 showed Steyer in third place at 20.2%, behind Xavier Becerra at 26.0% and Steve Hilton at 27.2%: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor
Steyer trailed Becerra by 330,495 votes in the official statewide count reviewed, making advancement to the general election unlikely: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

NBC's live results also showed Hilton first, Becerra second, and Steyer third, reinforcing the official statewide picture: https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

NBC's post-election coverage described California's governor race as unsettled but still had Hilton, Becerra, and Steyer in that order, with no sign of Steyer overtaking Becerra: https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-election/california-governor-key-races-house-unsettled-vote-count-continues-rcna348210

County-level AP snapshots did not show a clear late-count reservoir large enough to erase Steyer's large statewide deficit to Becerra: https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

Even with the newly reported formatting problem, the description only mentions tens of thousands of records in two large counties, which on its face still appears small relative to Steyer's 330,495-vote deficit unless the error is broader and highly favorable to him.

hypotheses.description: The market resolves NO: Tom Steyer does not win the 2026 California gubernatorial election, whether because he fails to advance from the top-two primary, loses the general election, withdraws, or otherwise is not the ultimately recognized winner.
supporting_evidence: California's official statewide results page showed Steyer in third place rather than in a top-two qualifying position: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor
Under California's top-two system, only the top two candidates advance, so Steyer's current third-place position is the dominant reason NO is favored: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/

Steyer's deficit to Becerra was very large relative to the remaining ballots, implying he would need an unusually favorable late-count break to advance: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/governor

NBC's live results and analysis both continued to show Steyer behind Becerra after additional counting, indicating no visible breakout in the past 48 hours reviewed: https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-primary-elections/california-governor-results

AP's live results likewise showed Hilton and Becerra ahead of Steyer and had not indicated a Steyer path into the top two: https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

The new audit evidence suggests the dashboard may have omitted some vote-center tallies, but it does not say cast ballots were affected and does not yet establish an error magnitude obviously sufficient to overturn Steyer's very large gap.
contradicting_evidence: Millions of ballots remained unprocessed, so the primary outcome was not yet certified and a comeback was still mathematically possible: https://dp.electionresults.sos.ca.gov/unprocessed-ballots-status
AP had not formally called which two candidates would advance at the time reviewed, preserving some uncertainty: https://apnews.com/projects/elections-2026/california-primary-results-governor/

Steyer had substantial resources and a real statewide campaign, which kept a small residual chance alive despite his current position: https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2026-election/tom-steyer-unexpected-alliance-progressives-california-governor-race-rcna342878

Pre-election polling suggested Steyer was at least competitive for a runoff spot before votes were fully counted: https://www.nbclosangeles.com/news/local/california-governor-poll-xavier-becerra-steve-hilton-tom-steyer/3897267/

New evidence reports an expedited audit over omitted cross-county vote-center tallies and says affected centers may be concentrated in metropolitan areas where Steyer invested heavily, which increases the chance that current unofficial standings understate his support.

updated_structured_forecast.hypothesis_to_forecast_mapping: Since H1=YES and H2=NO, yes_prob = posterior_probability(H1) directly. H1 posterior: 3%, therefore yes_prob = 3%.
updated_structured_forecast.rationale: The main update is that the new evidence introduces a specific, non-generic mechanism by which the unofficial statewide governor standings could change: an acknowledged ballot-export formatting error that omitted some cross-county vote-center tallies and is now under expedited audit. That raises Steyer's chances somewhat because a systematic reporting omission is more consequential than ordinary late counting, especially if affected metropolitan vote centers were relatively favorable to him. Even so, the prior state count had him trailing Becerra by 330,495 votes, and the new evidence only explicitly references tens of thousands of records in two counties rather than an error clearly large enough to erase that deficit. So YES moves up from a near-vanishing chance to a still-small tail outcome.

NEW EVIDENCE (received after your initial research):

SACRAMENTO, June 6 — California election officials said on Saturday they had opened an expedited audit after several counties reported a ballot-export formatting error that omitted some cross-county vote-center tallies from the statewide unofficial governor dashboard. The Secretary of State's office said the issue did not affect cast ballots themselves but could alter the standing of candidates as corrected files are uploaded during the canvass, with two large Southern California counties estimating that tens of thousands of records must be reprocessed. A Steyer campaign aide said the affected vote centers were concentrated in metropolitan areas where the candidate had invested heavily in same-day voter outreach.
---

Using ONLY the information above — your prior structured forecast plus this new evidence — update your world model and produce a revised forecast.

Rules:
- DO NOT search the web, use any tools, or look up any external information.
  Reason entirely from your existing forecast and the new evidence above.
- Update H1 and H2 posterior_probability to reflect what the new evidence implies.
- Update the supporting_evidence or contradicting_evidence list for the affected hypothesis to include a brief note about the new evidence.
- Update yes_prob (must equal H1 posterior_probability).
- Update rationale to explain concisely what changed and why.
- Keep all other fields (key_actors, key_mechanisms, etc.) unchanged.
- Output ONLY a valid JSON object in the exact same structure as the initial forecast above.  No markdown fences, no commentary before or after.
